overview: This image features a Ruffed Grouse (Bonasa umbellus) showcasing its prominent "ruff" of neck feathers. Reddit +1 The bird is likely a male performing a mating display, which typically involves fanning its tail and drumming its wings to produce a sound like a small engine starting. Reddit +1 Ruffed Grouse are known for their intricate mottled plumage, helping them blend into their forest habitat.

This image features a Ruffed Grouse (Bonasa umbellus) showcasing its prominent "ruff" of neck feathers.
The bird is likely a male performing a mating display, which typically involves fanning its tail and drumming its wings to produce a sound like a small engine starting.
Ruffed Grouse are known for their intricate mottled plumage, helping them blend into their forest habitat.
During winter, these birds are known to burrow into the snow for shelter and possess comb-like growths on their feet that act as snowshoes.
